parameters.subject: =Approval Request for {{ $json.Name }}'s Resignation
parameters.message:
=Dear {{ $json.Manager }},

You are requested your approval for the resignation of {{ $json.Name }}, Employee ID {{ $json['Emp ID'] }} from the {{ $json.Dept }} department. {{ $json['Name'].split(' ')[0] }} has a notice period of {{ $json['Notice Period (in Days)'] }} days and his last working day is set for {{ $json.LWD }}.

Approval is required to initiate the departmental clearance process.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sheet Link: Click here to view Sheet

value.EMP ID: ={{ $('Append or update row in sheet').item.json['Emp ID'] }}

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet1

parameters.subject: =Clearance Initiated – IT Action Required for Employee ID: {{ $json['Emp ID'] }}
parameters.message:
=

This is to inform you that the exit request for {{ $json.Name }} (Employee ID: {{ $json['Emp ID'] }}) has been formally approved by HR and the reporting manager.

The employee’s Last Working Day (LWD) is {{ $json.LWD }}.

Kindly initiate the IT clearance process and complete the following actions before the LWD:

  • Asset recovery (laptop, ID card, access devices, etc.)
  • System access deactivation
  • Software license revocation
  • Email and communication tool access review

Please update the clearance status in the system upon completion.
Timely completion of this process is essential to ensure smooth exit formalities.

values.fieldLabel: Are you sure you want to apply for resignation?

values.placeholder: Please describe your reason to apply for resignation

nodes.name: Append or update row in sheet2

parameters.subject: Resignation Process Started
parameters.message:
=
Process Initiated
Acknowledgment of Resignation

Dear {{ $('empData').item.json.Name }},

We have received your formal resignation submitted via the Exit Portal. This email confirms that your offboarding process has been initiated across the IT, Finance, HR, and Audit departments.

Employee ID: {{ $('empData').item.json['Emp ID'] }}
Full Notice Period: {{ $json["LWD"] }}

{{ $json.notice_flexibility == "Yes" ? `We note that your contract allows for flexible notice periods. While the standard end date listed above, your requested date of ${$json["requested_LWD"]} is currently being reviewed by HR for approval.` : `Per your employment contract, the standard notice period applies. Your tentative last working day is scheduled for ${$json["LWD"]}.` }}

Next Steps:

  • Clearance: You will receive automated approval requests from IT, Finance, and Audit.
  • Exit Interview: HR will reach out to schedule your final interview shortly.
  • Assets: Please ensure all company property is ready for handover on your final day.

We thank you for your contributions to the Pittie Group and wish to ensure a smooth transition for you.

Best Regards,
People Operations Team
Pittie Group

This is an automated message. Please contact HR if you have any questions regarding your final settlement.

parameters.subject: ={{ $('empData').item.json.Name }} has applied for resignation.

options.systemMessage:
You are an Event Feedback Form Question Generator.

Goal:
Given an event name and an event description, generate a concise list of 6–7 feedback questions that will be used to create a Google Form via an API.

Output rules (STRICT):
- Return ONLY a valid JSON object.
- The JSON must have exactly one top-level key: "questions".
- "questions" must be an array of 6–7 objects.
- Each question object MUST contain only these keys:
  1) "question_text"
  2) "question_type"
- Do NOT include any other keys (no ids, no options, no metadata, no extra wrappers).

Question quality rules:
- Questions must be relevant to the specific event described.
- Mix rating-style and specific open-ended questions.
- Avoid duplicates and keep wording clear and short.

Allowed question_type values (use only these):
- "rating"
- "short_text"
- "long_text"
- "multiple_choice"
- "checkboxes"

General guidance:
- Prefer "rating" for satisfaction/quality metrics.
- Use "long_text" for suggestions and improvements.
- Use "short_text" for quick factual answers (e.g., role, expectations met).
- Use "multiple_choice"/"checkboxes" only when the question can be answered without custom options; do not add options in output.

Return JSON only. No explanations.

=You are an HR Email Drafting Agent for Pittie Group. Goal: Generate a professional, mobile-friendly HTML email requesting feedback for an event the employee attended, with the Google Form link rendered as a prominent button, and Pittie Group branding (logo included). Branding: - Company name: Pittie Group - Logo URL (must be included in the HTML near the top): https://pittiegroup.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/cropped-Group-1.png Inputs you will receive: - toEmail - employeeFirstName - eventName - eventDescription - formResponsesLink Output rules (STRICT): - Return ONLY a valid JSON object in exactly this format and key order: { "toEmail": "...", "emailBody": "...", "emailSubject": "..." } - Do NOT include any other keys. - Do NOT wrap output in markdown or code fences. - Ensure JSON is valid: escape quotes inside HTML as needed, no trailing commas. Email subject rules: - Keep subject short and clear. - Must mention the eventName. HTML email rules: - emailBody MUST be valid HTML (inline CSS only; no external CSS, no
